patches.summary: Threaded context.Context through the generated HTTP client so Ctrl+C and command-level timeouts cancel in-flight requests.
patches.reason: Generated client built requests via http.NewRequest with context.Background(), so multi-hop commands (megatrend-map, sync) could hold the terminal hostage for the full HTTPClient.Timeout after SIGINT. Greptile P1 on PR #548.
patches.validated_outcome: go build/vet/test pass for the full module.
patches.upstream_issue: Generator template internal/generator/templates/client.go.tmpl emits http.NewRequest without ctx for every printed CLI; upstream fix would replace this single-CLI patch across the catalog.

patches.summary: Cap parsed trend BodyText by rune count rather than byte slice so the 4000-char limit doesn't split a multi-byte UTF-8 sequence introduced by html.UnescapeString.
patches.reason: Greptile flagged a U+FFFD replacement glyph at the byte boundary on long trend bodies. Adds a shared truncateRunes helper in thparse/common.go and uses it from ParseTrendPage.

patches.summary: Reject negative durations in parseSince (they silently inverted the lookback window to a future cutoff) and cap the first-run inbox scan at 200 trends instead of dumping the whole corpus.
patches.reason: Both flagged by Greptile on PR #548 as edge-case correctness/UX bugs: --since -7d returned empty results without an error, and a first inbox run on a freshly synced sitemap (hundreds of thousands of rows) would stream the entire corpus.

patches.summary: Source watch --since from the local store's first_seen rather than RSS PubDate; upsert all fetched category trends, then return the recency window from the store.
patches.reason: Category-card HTML has no per-trend publication dates, so the previous withinPubDateCutoff check was always lenient and --since was effectively a no-op. first_seen is the only reliable time signal we have for category-page sources, and it gives --since the obvious meaning of "trends discovered in my store in the last N time". Greptile blocking finding on PR #548.

patches.summary: Forward the --business profile into llmTrendScore so the LLM scores trends against the user's stated business context.
patches.reason: scoreTrends received business but never passed it to llmTrendScore, so scout --llm scored trends on an undirected scale unrelated to --business. Greptile blocking finding on PR #548.

patches.summary: Removed the always-empty keyword_overlap_with_megatrends field from megatrend-map output; updated SKILL.md/README.md/manifest description to match what the command actually returns (two-level related-trend walk).
patches.reason: Output schema advertised a list field that was hardcoded to []; the underlying overlap join isn't implemented. Documenting truth beats shipping a misleading schema. Greptile P2 on PR #548.

patches.summary: Added parsed TrendHunter parser/store packages and wired the advertised novel and sugar commands.
patches.reason: The generated help described these commands, but the command tree and backing parser/store packages were missing.
patches.validated_outcome: go build ./..., go vet ./..., and focused thparse/thstore tests pass with GOCACHE=/tmp/trendhunter-go-build; live TrendHunter commands are blocked in this sandbox by DNS resolution failure.
